hypothesis: Providing the LLM agent with local thermodynamic and kinetic sensitivity information from the simulator—specifically per-action gradients of equilibrium phase fractions with respect to temperature and composition plus short-time kinetic derivatives of phase fraction evolution—will increase its success rate on multi-phase synthesis targets (e.g., Challenge 3) by at least 50% and reduce the number of simulator calls needed to find a solution by at least 30% relative to the same LLM using only final-state simulator outputs.
